title: brossaea shallon
intro: Brossaea shallon, also known as Shallon, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly found in Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of limonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Brossaea shallon include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Brossaea shallon can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
